title: argyranthemum adauctum subsp. canariense
intro: Argyranthemum adauctum subsp. canariense, also known as Canary Island Daisy, is a flowering plant species native to the Canary Islands. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Argyranthemum adauctum subsp. canariense include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
